title: Daily Briefing — Apr 10
headline: Flows are bullish, but defensive sectors are leading — watch for rotation.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us. Broad market is firming up: SPY at $679.91 is holding above all major MAs, VIX is dropping below the SMA20, and Fear & Greed has pulled up to 37.2 from extreme lows. We're seeing a shift from panic to cautious optimism — but not a euphoric chase.

Sector rotation is clear: Utilities (XLU $47.15), Real Estate (XLRE $42.73), and Industrials (XLI $172.19) are leading, all breaking above 20 MA and showing strong momentum. Meanwhile, Energy (XLE $57.33) is weakening with elevated risk, and Discretionary (XLY $112.74) remains lagging despite today's pop. Tech is improving: look at NVDA ($183.91) and ARM ($149.79) both active above 20 MA, but MSFT ($373.07) is still below the 20 MA — don't chase.

Contrarian warning: defensive sector leadership with Financials (XLF $51.33) still elevated risk says the market isn't fully risk-on. Fear & Greed rising but still 'fear' (not extreme greed, so rule R013 is clear). VIX normal regime gives breathing room, but if XLF breaks SMA200 ($52.28), that's your canary. Abundance mindset: less trades, more wins — don't chase AMZN (+5.6%) after a run, let it mean revert.
callout_body:
SPY: $679.91 (20 MA $656.96 — above = bullish)
NVDA: $183.91 (20 MA $177.15 — active)
XLU: $47.15 (20 MA $46.01 — leading quadrant)
XLF: $51.33 (SMA200 $52.28 — risk trigger)
XLE: $57.33 (20 MA $59.39 — risk in energy)
VIX: 19.34 (Fear & Greed 37.2 (climbing out of extreme fear)
action_items: Monitor XLF close versus SMA200 ($52.28): breakdown triggers equity risk reduction per rule R001.
Watch tech leaders (NVDA, ARM, AAPL) — stay active only above 20 MA, exit if daily closes below.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Abundance mindset: next opportunity, not last chase.
